what_it_does: An archived (v1) presentation stylesheet that consumes the zone tokens from colors_and_type.css to style a 'tonal zoning' deliverable page: it styles the body with a radial-gradient ground plus a faint masked architectural grid, a masthead (eyebrow/title/lede/key), section scaffolding, a zone-registry card grid (each card showing a wash chip + role/name/desc/token list), a component-role demo row (buttons/chips/Vi pill vocabulary), and a full 'Overview dashboard' mock (left nav + main canvas with stats/canvas tiles/activity feed + right gold Vi rail) with ghosted off-frame continuation and spatial path layers.
observations: Header explicitly states 'Nothing here invents colour; it only composes the zone roles' — strict separation from the token layer.
All colors/spacing/radii/shadows/fonts are referenced via var(--…) tokens; no raw hex defined here.

Body background layers a radial-gradient of --zone-base-strong over --zone-base-surface; a fixed ::before draws a 64px dual-axis grid from --spatial-faint, masked by a radial gradient and scaled by --spatial-opacity.
Reuses the zone vocabulary: .ov-block.content/source/package, .cvx-chip--ready/pending/source/reject, .cvx-btn--primary/secondary/reject all map to zone-*-surface/edge/ink tokens.
The Overview mock is a fixed 1240px-wide, 3-column grid (232px nav / 1fr main / 320px Vi rail) with transform-origin top-left (built to be scaled).
Gold (--vi-surface/strong/edge, --accent-gold) is used for the Vi rail, active nav item, stat values, and the diamond 'gem' motif — consistent with gold-as-Vi-voice.
Spatial/connective layers (.ov-spatial, .ov-flow, .tz-ghost) are pointer-events:none decorative path/ghost layers driven by --spatial-opacity.
Uses CSS fallback custom-prop pattern e.g. var(--zone-ink, var(--accent-bronze)) so cards can be themed by setting --zone-ink locally.
Stat values rendered big and gold (font-display, --accent-gold), matching the deck observation noted in the token file.
standouts: A self-contained demonstration surface that renders the zone registry as a catalog (wash chips + token lists) AND applies it in a realistic dashboard mock in the same sheet.
Ghosted off-frame continuation panels (.tz-ghost) and connective path layers, signalling a spatial/branching workspace metaphor beyond a single screen.
Local --zone-ink / --zone-edge / --zone-surface override pattern lets a generic card pick up any zone's accent by setting one variable.
conventions: Compose-only: consumes tokens, never defines color (clean token-vs-presentation separation).
Token-driven: every value is a var() reference.
Zone vocabulary reuse: component states map to named zone roles, not ad-hoc colors.
Decorative layers are pointer-events:none and opacity-gated by a single --spatial-opacity knob.
concerns: Hardcoded geometry: many raw px sizes/positions and the fixed 1240px .ov width and 760px min-height are literal (presentation-mock dimensions, but not tokenised).
A few literal rgba()/percentage values in box-shadow composites (e.g. the extra 0 40px 80px -40px rgba(31,26,18,.28)) sit outside the token system.
Archived (_archive-v1) — superseded; a maintained consumer would need the v2 equivalent.
Large amount of mock-specific styling (.ov-*) tightly coupled to one demo dashboard layout rather than reusable components.
notes: The v1 (archived) presentation sheet that demonstrates the tonal-zoning system — it renders the zone registry as a catalog and applies it in a full Overview-dashboard mock, composing only from the tokens and never inventing color.
questions: Is the 1240px Overview mock meant to be a one-off demo or a template that later became real components?
What does the spatial/ghost/flow path layer represent semantically (branching workspaces? navigation continuity?) — it's drawn but its data source isn't shown here.
Why archived — did the zoning presentation move into the React component layer in v2?
purpose_vs_actual: Matches: header declares it a deliverable-presentation sheet that composes zone roles, and the file does exactly that (catalog + applied dashboard mock) using only tokens.
intention_signals: Inferred: this is the showcase/proof surface for the tonal-zoning language — it exists to demonstrate that the nine functional zones, the gold Vi voice, and the spatial connective layers actually compose into a coherent real-looking product screen, validating the token model by applying it end-to-end in a dashboard with nav, content zones, a source/activity feed, and a Vi recommendation rail.
novelty: Combining a token-catalog view and a fully applied dashboard mock in one composition-only sheet, with ghosted off-frame continuation and connective path layers expressing a spatial workspace, is more than ordinary demo CSS.
contribution: Brings the applied vocabulary layer: how zone tokens render as catalog cards, the shared component vocabulary (.cvx-btn/.cvx-chip/.cvx-vi mapped to zone roles), and a concrete dashboard composition (nav / zoned content / source feed / gold Vi rail) plus the spatial-continuation and grid-ground motifs — a reference for how the tonal-zoning model becomes a real screen.
summary_for_embedding: Archived v1 tonal-zoning presentation CSS that composes the design system's zone tokens into a masthead, a zone-registry card catalog, a shared button/chip/Vi-pill vocabulary, and a full 1240px Overview-dashboard mock (nav, zoned content blocks, source activity feed, gold Vi rail) with masked grid ground and ghosted off-frame continuation layers.
intention_for_embedding: Demonstrate and validate the tonal-zoning language by composing its tokens end-to-end into a realistic product dashboard, proving the nine zones, the gold Vi voice, and the spatial connective layers cohere as a real screen.
